event_blocks.summary: The Predator World 8-Ball Championship, held in San Juan, Puerto Rico, from November 19-22, 2022, commenced with a quarter-final match between Jason Shaw of the UK and Alex Pagulayan of the Philippines, played under WPA rules with a race to 10 and call shot. The match began with both players at 0 points. Shaw, nicknamed 'Eagle Eye,' executed a powerful break shot, scattering the balls, with a referee in a black and yellow striped shirt inspecting the table. Commentators Marcus Chamat and an announcer, identified as 'Mark,' provided analysis, discussing prize money, the '60-second shot clock,' and Shaw's precise technique and deliberate pace around the blue-felt pool table. The professional arena featured branded banners, blue patterned carpet, and an attentive, partially masked audience. Shaw meticulously assessed the table layout, retrieved a pocketed ball, and prepared for his next shot, demonstrating intense focus as the commentators continued their play-by-play and banter.

He is explicitly identified by commentators as 'Jason Shaw', whose play is praised for its 'perfect pace' around the table.

Pool Table.description: A professional blue-felt pool table with black rails, branded with the 'Predator' logo, set on a blue patterned carpet in a tournament arena, shown from multiple angles including overhead and side views during the break and post-break assessment.

Referee.description: Dressed in a black and yellow striped shirt with white gloves, inspecting the table after the break shot, standing near the pool table, visible during the post-break assessment phase.

The scoreboard indicates a 1-1 tie between Shaw and Pagulayan in the 'Race to 10' format of the Predator World 8-Ball Championship in San Juan, Puerto Rico. As Shaw takes his position and lines up a shot under the Shot Clock, which counts down from 20 seconds, two male commentators discuss his strategy, with one suggesting he's "weighing his options" and will "draw back off this... for the eight into the same pocket." Shaw executes a precise stroke, and the distinct sound of the cue ball hitting the rack is heard, followed by the "clack" of a ball dropping into a pocket. The commentators react positively, noting "Nicely done" and "Nicely played." The camera dynamically follows the action, including an overhead view of the scattered balls. The Referee, dressed in a black and yellow striped shirt with white gloves, inspects the table. Shaw then walks away from the table, and another shot is heard, followed by a "clack" as a ball goes in, prompting a commentator to exclaim, "And in it goes! Jason Shaw then going back into the lead, 2-1." The scoreboard updates to 2-1 in Shaw's favor. The commentator then announces a short break, saying, "we're gonna go, but not for very long. See you soon." The scene transitions to promotional graphics for the CSI Predator US Pro Billiard Series, highlighting upcoming 2022 events such as the Medalla Light Puerto Rico Open, the Predator World 8-Ball Championship, and the Predator World Junior 9-Ball Championships, all part of the Caribbean CSI Expo in San Juan, Puerto Rico. Throughout this transition, an upbeat, rhythmic background music track plays, interspersed with the sounds of pool balls being hit and racked, creating a dynamic and engaging atmosphere. The audience, seated behind barriers, watches attentively throughout the match.

event_blocks.summary: Promotional graphics for upcoming billiard events, including the 'Predator World Junior 9-Ball Championships' and the 'CSI Predator US Pro Billiard Series,' featuring the panther logo and CSI sponsorship, played with upbeat electronic music and female vocals, transitioned to live action at the 'Predator World 8-Ball Championship' in San Juan. Alex Pagulayan, wearing a light shirt and black gloves, executed a powerful break shot on the blue-felt pool table, scattering the balls with a loud crack, though no ball went down. Shaw, in a dark long-sleeve shirt with sponsor logos, then took his turn, focusing intently under the Shot Clock, which counted down from 20 seconds. The scoreboard showed 'Race to 10' with Pagulayan at 1 and Shaw at 2. Commentators discussed Pagulayan's unbeaten run and Shaw's path to the quarter-finals. Pagulayan, trailing 1-2, chalked his cue and prepared his shot, with commentators observing him 'weighing his opening shot.' The professional environment was highlighted by branded banners and focused lighting. Pagulayan executed shots, with commentators noting his 'awkward' position and 'struggling at the moment,' but also praising his 'clever shot' and calling him a 'very complete player.' Despite concerns about a potential injury affecting his mobility, he continued to play. Shaw was noted for his 'straight pool' and described as a 'really good all-round player,' with commentators expressing excitement over his 'tremendous' shots. The score progressed to Shaw leading 3-1, with commentators noting both players were 'kind of edgy.' The Referee, in a black and yellow striped shirt, inspected the table before Shaw lined up his break shot, which resulted in another 'dry' break. Pagulayan, identified as 'Alex,' a 'fun character' nicknamed 'the Lion,' assessed his shots, with commentators observing him 'struggling a little bit with the pace.' The match continued with Shaw leading 3-1, and Pagulayan trailing, with commentators discussing strategy and Pagulayan's 'twitching' style, comparing him to 'Alex Higgins.' Shaw was noted for his precise shots, with commentators discussing potential safety plays and intelligent shots. The score reached 4-1 in favor of Shaw, with commentators expressing concern for Pagulayan, emphasizing the high stakes of the quarter-final match in the World Championship.

event_blocks.summary: The Predator World 8-Ball Championship features intense competition, with Jason Shaw holding a commanding 5-1 lead against Pagulayan in a race to 10. Shaw, clad in a dark long-sleeve shirt with 'nineball' and 'Predator' logos, demonstrates focused precision under the pressure of the Shot Clock, executing powerful breaks and strategic shots, often praised by commentators for his 'lovely compact action' and 'minimal Q action, minimal movement.' The blue-felt Predator pool table, illuminated in the dimly lit arena, serves as the focal point, with dynamic camera angles capturing every ball's movement. Commentators provide live analysis, discussing prize funds, tournament schedules, and other ongoing matches, including those involving Sanchez Ruiz, Conrad Musician, Mario Hee, Daniel Maciel, Voitech Shevchek, and Victor Zelensky. The atmosphere is amplified by the attentive audience, branded banners, and sponsor logos such as 'Rums of Puerto Rico' and 'Jam Up Apparel.' Meanwhile, promotional graphics for Predator products like the Apex Next Generation Billiard Table, Arena Billiard Light, and Arcos II Precision Calibrated Billiard Balls, along with announcements for the CSI Predator US Pro Billiard Series and upcoming events like the Predator World 10-Ball Championship, are interspersed throughout the broadcast, accompanied by calm, rhythmic background music.

Pagulayan.description: Alex 'Pagulayan', a player from the Philippines, wearing a light-colored short-sleeve shirt and black gloves, is observed walking around the blue-felt pool table, assessing his shot, and preparing to strike with his cue under the Shot Clock, displaying focus and strategic deliberation. Commentators refer to him by his first name, Alex, noting he 'can't be giving these games away anymore' given the score, and discuss his aiming for the 'nine' ball and 'shooting the 12', with one commentator reacting 'Wow' to a shot and later speculating he might 'give up the match'.

raw_segments: The segment opens at the Predator World 8-Ball Championship, where player Jason Shaw, in a dark long-sleeve shirt with sponsor logos, executes a precise shot on the blue-felt Predator pool table as the Shot Clock counts down. Commentators provide live commentary, with one noting Shaw is "not a world champion yet" and the other confirming a successful shot with "and it goes." They also announce the current score as "leading 4-1" before stating they will "nip out for a couple of minutes." The audience watches intently, and a Referee in a black and yellow striped shirt inspects the table. Following Shaw's turn, the scene transitions to a series of promotional graphics for Predator products, including the Arena Billiard Light, Aerorack Professional Billiard Rack, Arcadia High Performance Billiard Cloth, Arcos II Precision Calibrated Billiard Balls, and the Apex Next Generation Billiard Table, each displayed with a QR code and the Predator logo. This promotional sequence is accompanied by a calm, rhythmic background music track. The segment concludes with a graphic for the CSI Predator US Pro Billiard Series, highlighting upcoming 2022 and 2023 events, including the Predator World 10-Ball Championship in Las Vegas.

The segment opens with a promotional graphic for the 'Apex Next Generation Billiard Table' by Predator, featuring a QR code, accompanied by a gentle, ethereal background music. The scene then transitions to the live action of the Predator World 8-Ball Championship, where a male commentator warmly welcomes viewers back to the convention center in San Juan, Puerto Rico. On a professional blue-felt pool table, branded with the Predator logo, Shaw, wearing a dark long-sleeve shirt with sponsor logos and a black glove, executes a powerful break shot, with the distinct sound of balls scattering. The scoreboard shows Pagulayan 1, Shaw 4, in a 'Race to 10' format, as the commentator notes, "Shaw breaking, he's 4-1 up. Big break." The camera captures multiple angles as Shaw assesses the table, and the commentator exclaims, "Any ball? 11? Yes, last second!" followed by the clear sound of a ball dropping into a pocket. The commentator further describes it as a "last second entry," noting the "five ball coming down and making it in this corner." Shaw holds his hand up, which the commentator interprets as a sign of luck, adding, "he'll take it." The Shot Clock is visible, counting down from 20 seconds during Shaw’s turn, while a small audience, some wearing masks, watches intently. The dimly lit environment, with banners displaying 'Predator World 8-Ball Championship' and sponsor logos, focuses attention on the brightly lit table. Shaw walks around, evaluating his next shot, as the commentator observes that there aren't many people in the arena yet, but it's early in the event. The 8-ball remains in play, with balls strategically scattered, maintaining a tense and competitive atmosphere.

The video opens with an overhead view of a professional blue-felt pool table at the Predator World 8-Ball Championship, showing balls scattered and the scoreboard indicating Shaw leading Pagulayan 4-1 in a race to 10. A male commentator's voice is heard, humorously suggesting that "pool fans" might have been "out playing last night" in "San Juan," setting a relaxed, late-night tournament atmosphere. The Shot Clock appears on screen, counting down from 20 seconds as Shaw, wearing a dark long-sleeve shirt with 'nineball' and 'Predator' logos, prepares for his shot. The commentator then details the tournament schedule, announcing the "first semi-final" at "1 PM local time" and "the final at 7 PM tonight." Shaw walks around the table, assessing angles, then takes a focused stance, executing a precise shot that sends the cue ball into a cluster of balls, causing several to move. The commentator remarks, "Yeah, that's a nice little shot to get to the two," directly referencing Shaw's play. The camera follows the action, switching between overhead and side angles, capturing the movement of the balls. After the shot, the camera returns to an overhead view of the new ball positions, then cuts to Shaw walking away, adjusting his glove, as the Shot Clock resets. The audience is visible in the background, observing intently in the dimly lit arena, which features branded banners for the Predator World 8-Ball Championship and sponsors like Medalla Light and Alfa. The commentator concludes by thanking "sponsors Medalla Light," "Kamui," "Alpha Coin," "Seeburt.com," "CSI," "Predator Cues," and "Jam Up Apparel," reinforcing the professional tournament environment.

The Predator World 8-Ball Championship unfolds with player Shaw, dressed in a dark long-sleeve shirt adorned with 'nineball' and 'Predator' logos, intensely focused on his turn. Commentators immediately highlight the high stakes, discussing the substantial "$250,000" prize fund. Shaw, with visible tattoos on his left arm and a black glove on his left hand, prepares and executes shots under the pressure of the Shot Clock, which counts down from 20 seconds. The distinct sound of his cue stick hitting a ball is heard, prompting one commentator to exclaim, "Oh, that's a nice shot," followed by another adding, "Nicely done. Landed perfectly on this mark." The camera alternates between side and overhead views of the blue-felt Predator pool table, showing balls repositioned after each shot. The scoreboard indicates Shaw leading 4-1 against Pagulayan in a 'Race to 10'. Another shot sound rings out as commentators analyze Shaw's strategy, noting a "run through for the four in the side" and questioning, "can he get to the one here?" They continue to discuss ball positions, mentioning "that four is laying in the side pocket" and "looking at five, one." The audience watches intently from behind barriers, adding to the live event atmosphere, with large event banners visible in the backdrop. The segment concludes with Shaw lining up a critical shot, the camera zooming in on his focused expression and precise cue alignment, as a final shot sound is heard and a commentator remarks, "this is looking ominous."

The segment opens with a close-up of the blue-felt Predator pool table, showing balls scattered after a break during the Predator World 8-Ball Championship. Shaw, wearing a dark long-sleeve shirt with 'nineball' and 'Predator' logos, black pants, and a black glove, is seen taking his turn. Commentators describe his focused approach, with one noting his 'eagle eye swooping, soaring' as he carefully lines up a shot, while the Shot Clock counts down from 20 seconds. The distinct sound of the cue striking the ball and balls rolling is heard as Shaw executes a shot. A commentator exclaims, 'Oh, that's a great shot,' as one ball is pocketed, updating the score to Shaw 5, Pagulayan 1. Shaw then stands, walks around the table, and prepares for his next shot, displaying his focused and precise technique, which a commentator praises as 'lovely compact action' with 'minimal Q action, minimal movement.' Another successful shot is heard and seen, leading to the score of '5-1,' with a commentator noting Shaw is 'halfway to the 10 in the first of our quarter-finals.' The referee, dressed in a black and yellow striped shirt, inspects the table. The camera then pulls back to a wide-angle view of the dimly lit arena, revealing multiple pool tables, the audience seated behind barriers, and prominent event branding for the 'Predator World 8-Ball Championship' and 'Rums of Puerto Rico.' The scoreboard consistently displays 'Race to 10' and the current score. Commentary shifts to discussing other ongoing matches, mentioning 'Sanchez Ruiz, 3-2 against Conrad Musician' and 'Mario Hee, 5-0 ahead,' before the segment ends with a wide shot of the full venue, emphasizing the scale of the championship with other tables in use and event staff moving around.

The minute opens with a wide-angle view of the Predator World 8-Ball Championship arena, showcasing multiple pool tables on a tiered stage under dark, atmospheric lighting. The scoreboard prominently indicates Shaw leading Pagulayan 5-1 in a 'Race to 10' match. Commentators discuss other ongoing matches, noting Daniel Maciel is '5-0 up' and an 'all Polish affair' between Voitech Shevchek, who is '4-2 down,' and Victor Zelensky. They reflect on a prediction for a repeat of the International Nine-ball Open Championship final between 'Shaw and Zelensky,' pondering if it could happen in the 8-ball format. The camera then cuts to a close-up of Shaw, a player in a dark long-sleeve shirt with 'nineball' and 'Predator' sponsor logos, displaying intense focus. The scene transitions to the main blue-felt Predator table, where a referee in a black and yellow striped shirt inspects the racked balls. Shaw takes his break shot, striking the cue ball with precision, scattering the balls across the table. Dynamic overhead and side camera angles capture the action, including Shaw’s deliberate follow-through. The audience, some wearing masks, watches attentively behind barriers. A green Shot Clock counts down from 20 seconds on screen during Shaw’s turn. After the break, the balls are scattered, and Shaw steps back to assess the table. Commentators remark on the competitive stakes, stating, 'Time will tell these few hours. These guys are battling it out for first prize and the World Championship title.' Another adds, 'Yeah, actually saying that, it can't work like that because they would meet in the semis if both win, I believe.' The commentator then identifies the player as 'Jason,' noting, 'Dry break from Jason again,' and observes, 'there's not been many balls made, has there?' followed by 'No, on the breaks? And it's a good break, you know.' The branding of the Predator World 8-Ball Championship, along with sponsor logos like 'Rums of Puerto Rico' and 'Jam Up Apparel', remains prominently displayed, emphasizing the professional and competitive environment.

The segment opens at the Predator World 8-Ball Championship, where Pagulayan, wearing a light-colored short-sleeve shirt and black gloves, is actively engaged in his turn. Commentator 1 notes it was "a little good break" and remarks that "Alex knows he can't be giving these games away anymore," highlighting Pagulayan trailing Shaw 1-5 in a race to 10. Pagulayan walks around the blue-felt pool table, assessing the scattered balls after the break, as the Shot Clock counts down from 15 seconds, with the commentator adding, "time is running out." He carefully positions himself, takes a shot, and the camera follows the cue ball’s trajectory as it strikes multiple balls, including the 8-ball, which remains in play. An overhead view reveals the strategic positioning of the balls, with Pagulayan’s shot resulting in a successful pocketing of a solid-colored ball. He stands up, adjusts his grip, and walks around the table again, contemplating his next move, as the commentator identifies him aiming for the "nine" ball. An official announcement, "Attention, attention please," briefly cuts through the commentary. The commentator then observes a shot "doesn't go in the corner" and suggests Pagulayan "needs to break him up here," before identifying him "shooting the 12." The distinct sound of a cue striking a ball is heard, followed by a commentator's "Wow." The audience, seated behind barriers, watches intently, adding to the competitive atmosphere. The branding of the Predator World 8-Ball Championship is prominent on banners and table rails, with sponsor logos like 'Rums of Puerto Rico' and 'Jam Up Apparel' visible. Pagulayan’s focused demeanor and deliberate movements reflect the high stakes of the match, as the commentator concludes by speculating, "He's gonna give up the match," followed by another brief, unclear comment, "I can't."

event_blocks.summary: In the Predator World 8-Ball Championship, Alex Pagulayan, clad in a light-colored short-sleeve shirt and black gloves, reacted with visible frustration after a shot, while the scoreboard indicated Jason Shaw held a commanding 5-1 lead in the 'Race to 10'. Commentators discussed Alex's condition, noting his leg injury caused him "total pain" and led him to consider withdrawing days prior, with one commentator stating, "He can't walk. He's given up the match," and another adding, "He feel it's not fair for himself." Jason Shaw, poised by the blue-felt pool table amidst scattered balls, was described as an "honest player" who did not wish to win under these circumstances. A referee, in a black and yellow striped shirt, briefly inspected the table before Pagulayan retrieved his cue, adjusted his grip, and chalked. Pagulayan then executed a precise shot, his movements and the ball's trajectory captured by the camera in the dimly lit arena adorned with Predator Championship branding. The sequence concluded with Pagulayan stepping back, Shaw awaiting his turn, and commentators offering "condolences to Alex" as Pagulayan was forced to retire injured, allowing Shaw to advance to the semi-final. The broadcast also promoted other quarter-final and semi-final matches, directing viewers to online platforms, and showed an overhead view of multiple pool tables in the arena, highlighting the tournament's scale and professional atmosphere.

Simultaneously, two male commentators discuss a player named Alex, with one deeper voice stating, "He can't walk. He's given up the match," and the other, with a slight accent, adding, "He feel it's not fair for himself." The commentators reveal Alex's leg is in too much pain, and that 'Jason Shaw' (who is visually seen standing poised by the blue-felt Predator pool table, observing the scattered balls after the break) doesn't want to win under such circumstances, praising him as a "very honest player." As the camera pulls back to a wide shot of the table, the referee, in a black and yellow striped shirt, briefly inspects it. Pagulayan then walks to his side, retrieves his cue, adjusts his grip, and chalks, while the commentators continue to express sympathy for Alex, noting he was in "total pain" and had considered withdrawing days prior. Pagulayan leans in and takes a precise shot, his movement and the ball’s trajectory followed by the camera, all set against the dimly lit arena with prominent Predator Championship branding. The sequence concludes with Pagulayan stepping back after his shot, maintaining the competitive tension, as Shaw waits his turn, while the commentators offer their "condolences to Alex."

The video opens with a close-up of Alex Pagulayan, wearing a light-colored short-sleeve shirt and black gloves, holding his cue stick and gesturing animatedly toward the audience and his opponent, Jason Shaw, who is dressed in a dark long-sleeve shirt with sponsor logos including 'nineball' and 'Predator'. Commentators explain that Pagulayan's gestures are a reaction to his injury, as he "has to retire injured" from the match, allowing Jason Shaw to "advance to the semi-final." The camera then pulls back to a wider view of the Predator World 8-Ball Championship stage, showing the blue-felt pool table with scattered balls, the referee in a black and yellow striped shirt inspecting the table, and the audience seated behind barriers. The large banner behind the table prominently displays 'PREDATOR WORLD 8-BALL CHAMPIONSHIP' with the stylized panther logo. The commentators continue, apologizing for the match's early end and promoting other ongoing "quarter-finals" and future semi-finals, directing viewers to their Facebook page or website. The scene transitions to an overhead shot revealing multiple pool tables in the arena, with players and referees at each, indicating a multi-table tournament setup. The lighting is dim, focused on the tables, creating a professional and competitive atmosphere, with the engaged audience, some wearing masks, adding to the live event ambiance as the commentators conclude their broadcast, thanking viewers and promising to return for the first semi-final at 1 PM.
